whatThisIs: Build an Adrogue-Madias correction plan for hypo- or hypernatremia. Sophie Well takes the patient weight, sex, measured sodium, chosen infusate (3% saline, 0.9% NS, 0.45% NS, D5W, or oral free water), and a 24-hour sodium target, then returns the expected change per litre of that infusate and the rate needed to hit the target without exceeding the conventional 8-10 mEq/L per 24 h ceiling. The output flags rates that would overcorrect and reminds you that ongoing urine losses can shift the plan within hours.
whenToUse: Reach for this any time you are about to write a correction order for symptomatic or severe dysnatremia and want to sanity-check the rate before it goes in the pump. It is a planning aid, not a substitute for hourly sodium re-checks - osmotic demyelination from overcorrected hyponatremia and cerebral edema from over-correction of chronic hypernatremia are both iatrogenic and both happen when teams stop measuring. Pair the planner with serial Na, urine output, and fluid-balance checks every 2-4 hours and reset the trajectory when reality drifts from the plan.
